name: standards-refresh.sh zero-rule refetch leaves the previously written source file byte-identical instead of clobbering it
command:
OUT=/tmp/cl564-r31-$$; mkdir -p $OUT/out && cat > $OUT/good.html <<'HTML'

Doc

Rule one

prose one.

HTML printf '

upstream page emptied

' > $OUT/empty.html cat > $OUT/reg.yaml </dev/null && cp $OUT/out/european-union/r31-src.yaml $OUT/orig.yaml && sed 's|good.html|empty.html|' $OUT/reg.yaml > $OUT/reg2.yaml && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/standards-refresh.sh" --registry $OUT/reg2.yaml --force --now 2026-07-12T00:00:00Z --out-dir $OUT/out 2>e2.err; rc=$?; [ $rc -eq 1 ] && grep -q 'INSUFFICIENT sid=r31-src' e2.err && cmp -s $OUT/out/european-union/r31-src.yaml $OUT/orig.yaml && echo zero-rule-refetch-preserves-file 1>&2; rm -rf $OUT
